steps.title: Extract center and radius from circle M
steps.content: The equation $(x-3)^{2}+(y-6)^{2}=4$ is in standard circle form $(x-h)^{2}+(y-k)^{2}=r^{2}$. The center is $(3,6)$ and the radius squared is 4, so the radius is $\sqrt{4}=2$.

steps.title: Apply constraints to find circle P's equation
steps.content:
Circle $P$ has the same center $(3,6)$ but its radius is twice circle $M$'s radius: $r_P = 2 \times 2 = 4$.

The equation for circle $P$ is:
$$(x-3)^{2}+(y-6)^{2}=4^{2}=16$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
